phase3_findings.description: The newline guard I wrote (`'\n' in NEW or '\r' in NEW`) is itself a validator whose accepted-input set is a SUPERSET of its consumer's contract (`str.splitlines()`). Eight further separators (\v, \f, \x1c-\x1e, NEL, LS, PS) pass the guard and still split the checkbox row, injecting a phantom `- [x]` AC row at exit 0. With a --note present both post-merge guards are skipped, so the row check was the only defense.

`_is_single_line`'s docstring asserted 'Every reader of a workpad section splits rows with str.splitlines()'. False: `_split_details` and `_append_progress_note` use `.split('\n')`. An absolute claim false against HEAD is a documented falsehood, so it re-opened the diff at any threshold despite the Suggestion grade.

`--rewrite-ac` argparse help omitted the new single-line rule — a user-facing contract for a rule that now aborts the call. Trivial and clearly correct to add, on the same surface that already drifted once.
An empty or whitespace-only `--rewrite-ac` NEW rewrites an AC row to blank text (exit 0, PATCHed).
The SKILL.md coupled-invariant pin used grep -q and matched 2 lines, so it guarded wholesale removal but not a partial revert of one of the two coupled sentences.

fix_decisions.claim_text: an empty or whitespace-only --rewrite-ac NEW rewrites an AC row to blank text
fix_decisions.evidence: Real but unfixed in-loop, and NOT claimed out-of-scope: this PR's diff touches the --rewrite-ac block, so the widens-surface guard would rightly disqualify an out-of-scope skip. It is pre-existing behavior (present before issue #338 and unchanged by it), it cannot launder a (post-merge) deferral (the blank text ends with no marker, so `_ends_with_post_merge` is False and no row transitions untagged -> tagged), and issue #338's scope is the retag rationale channel, not AC-row content validation. Revisit when AC-row content validation is tightened, or if a run is observed blanking a criterion.

fix_decisions.claim_text: state backstop residual limitations (ticked-row shuttle; net-zero remove-one/add-one)
fix_decisions.evidence: Up-calibrated, not down. Observable fail-direction: the guard FAILS OPEN — both shapes were reproduced against the real CLI shipping a (post-merge) deferral with no --note at exit 0 with a PATCH issued. Both also falsified an absolute claim the same diff publishes in SKILL.md and DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW ('a crafted multi-pair sequence that net-adds a (post-merge) row is caught by the same rule'), making each a documented falsehood on top of a fail-open guard. Blast radius is the audit trail the whole issue exists to create. Both fixed; the residual-limitations comment was deleted because the residuals are gone.

step_3_5_gate.result: clean after re-fix: no Critical/Important; 3 Suggestions, 2 applied (documented falsehood + help omission), 1 deferred with recorded evidence

park_calibration_gate.result: 2 parked findings re-graded and routed back (ticked-row shuttle -> iter 2; net-zero swap -> iter 3); 1 parked pin under-specification fixed in iter 4 (negative pin); 1 finding deferred with recorded evidence (degenerate NEW). NOT clean — re-grades are stated plainly in the verdict.
